title: docker-compose.yml for local development
description: Provide a development-time docker-compose setup that runs the server with hot-reload against source (not the production multi-stage image), mounting a local volume for the SQLite file and reading env vars from `.env.local`.
acceptanceCriteria: `docker-compose.yml` at repo root defines a single service running the server in dev mode
Service mounts the repo as a bind volume for hot-reload of source changes
Service mounts a named volume or local folder for the SQLite database file, persisting across `docker-compose down`/`up`
Service reads env vars from `.env.local` via `env_file`
`docker-compose up` starts the stack and the health-check endpoint responds within a reasonable startup time
`docker-compose down` followed by `docker-compose up` preserves previously created data (e.g. the bootstrap admin is not recreated with a duplicate)

steps.details: In `docker-compose.yml`, define service `app` using a lightweight dev Dockerfile target (or the `builder` stage from TASK-6 run with a dev command instead of the compiled `dist`), running `pnpm --filter @systembook/server dev`.

Bind-mount the repo root into the container for hot-reload. Add a named volume `sqlite-data` mounted at the directory containing `DATABASE_PATH` so the database persists independently of the bind mount.
Set `env_file: .env.local` on the service. Map container `PORT` to the same host port for local browser access.

Run `docker-compose up`, confirm health-check responds and the bootstrap admin exists. Run `docker-compose down` then `docker-compose up` again, and confirm the admin user was not duplicated (seed script from TASK-4 is idempotent).

technicalNotes: This compose file is for local development only — TASK-59 covers the separate production-reference compose file using the built image from TASK-58.
Do not commit `.env.local` — `docker-compose.yml` should reference it via `env_file` so real secrets never enter the repo.
